LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS MICHAEL VRIHEAS AND CECELIA VRIHEAS, AS TRUSTEES AND MICHAEL VRIHEAS' AN INDIVIDUAL'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION ADEQUATELY ALLEGES THAT PLAINTIFF VIOLATED AN IMPLIED TERM IN THE PROMISSORY NOTES THAT THE PAYMENTS MADE TO HIM PER THOSE NOTES WOULD BE FROM MONIES THAT WERE ENTIRELY THE PROPERTY OF DEFENDANTS AND DEFENDANTS BREACHED THAT IMPLIED TERM BY MAKING PAYMENTS FROM MONIES THAT WERE PARTIALLY THE PROPERTY OF THE PLAINTIFF. WHILE DEFENDANTS CONTEND THAT THESE ALLEGATIONS CAN'T BE TRUE, THE COURT IS REQUIRED TO ACCEPT THE TRUTH OF THE ALLEGATIONS ON A DEMURRER. THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS DOES NOT BAR THIS CLAIM BECAUSE IT IS ALLEGED THAT THE PAYMENTS ARE ONGOING. THE RULINGS ON THE PRIOR DEMURRER DO NOT BAR THIS CLAIM BECAUSE THOSE RULINGS DO NOT NECESSARILY PRECLUDE PLAINTIFF FROM PURSUING THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION. NOR DO THE ONE ACTION RULE OR THE RULE AGAINST DEFICIENCY JUDGMENTS NECESSARILY BAR THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION. PROCEDURALLY, THE ALLEGATIONS ABOUT SECURITY FOR THE PROMISSORY NOTES ARE VAGUE AND SO THE COURT IS NOT IN A POSITION TO KNOW WHETHER THERE IS SECURITY FOR EACH NOTE AND WHAT THAT SECURITY IS. SUBSTANTIVELY, LIBERALLY CONSTRUED THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION DOES NOT ALLEGE A DEFAULT IN THE REQUIRED PAYMENTS OF THE NOTES, BUT INSTEAD ALLEGES A "BREACH" REGARDING THE SOURCE OF THE PAYMENTS. DEFENDANTS HAVE NOT CITED ANY AUTHORITY, NOR IS THE COURT AWARE OF ANY, HOLDING THAT THE USUAL "SECURITY FIRST" RULES APPLY IN THIS UNUSUAL SITUATION. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: REYES F. HUNTER, CSR #6576, BUS. TEL: 530-339-5023. (D302)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-15-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Attorney, Thomas H. Kawaii, Esq. (650-260-3060) appears for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, John Wiley, Esq. (415-831-0500) appears for defendants/moving parties, Michael and Cecilia Vriheas

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides. The Court confirms the tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS MICHAEL VRIHEAS and CECELIA VRIHEAS, as TRUSTEES and MICHAEL VRIHEAS' an individual's DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is OVERRULED. The seventh cause of action adequately alleges that plaintiff violated an implied term in the promissory notes that the payments made to him per those notes would be from monies that were entirely the property of defendants and defendants breached that implied term by making payments from monies that were partially the property of the plaintiff. While defendants contend that these allegations can't be true, the court is required to accept the truth of the allegations on a demurrer. The statute of limitations does not bar this claim because it is alleged that the payments are ongoing. The rulings on the prior demurrer do not bar this claim because those rulings do not necessarily preclude plaintiff from pursuing the seventh cause of action. Nor do the one action rule or the rule against deficiency judgments necessarily bar the seventh cause of action. Procedurally, the allegations about security for the promissory notes are vague and so the court is not in a position to know whether there is security for each note and what that security is. Substantively, liberally construed the seventh cause of action does not allege a default in the required payments of the notes, but instead alleges a "breach" regarding the source of the payments. Defendants have not cited any authority, nor is the court aware of any, holding that the usual "security first" rules apply in this unusual situation. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR #6576, Bus. Tel: 530-339-5023. (D302)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-24-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. THOMAS H. KAWAII, ESQ., Tel No. 650-260-3060 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. JOHN WILEY, ESQ., Tel No. 415-831-0500 appearing for the Defendants Michael Vriheas and Cecelia Vriheas, Trustees.

RULING - Matter argued only as to the number of days leave to amend. The Court modifies its tentative ruling only as to the number of days leave to amend, and orders as follows: Demurrer sustained, with 27 days leave to amend. The court grants Defendants' demurrer to the first amended complaint, with 27 days leave to amend, as to the first, second, third, fourth, fifth, eighth, and ninth causes of action. In order to rely on the discovery rule for delayed accrual of a cause of action, a plaintiff whose complaint shows on its face that his claim would be time-barred without the benefit of the discovery rule must specifically plead facts to show (1) the time and manner of discovery and (2) the inability to have made earlier discovery despite reasonable diligence. (See Fox v. Ethicon Endo-Synergy, Inc. (2005) 35 Cal.4th 797.) Plaintiff is granted 27 days leave to amend to allege facts that despite a diligent investigation to the circumstances of the November 28, 2009, sale, he could not have discovered facts supporting the aforementioned causes of action. Defendants' demurrer to the sixth cause of action for breach of contract is sustained, with 27 days leave to amend, so that Plaintiff can plead facts alleging a breach of the contract's terms. Defendants' special demurrer under CCP section 430.10(d), is sustained, with 27 days leave to amend, to provide Plaintiff with the opportunity to name the lender East West Bank as a defendant. East West Bank is an indispensable party in the action because in order to return the property located at 3320 18th Street, San Francisco, California, to Plaintiff, the court would need to rescind East West Bank's the deed of trust on the property. Under CCP section 379(b), "it is not necessary that each defendant be interested as to every cause of action or as to all relief prayed for," for the party to be joined as a defendant. (CCP section 379(b).) Because Plaintiff seeks rescission of the contracts of sale, Defendants' special demurrer under CCP section 430.10(g) is also sustained with 27 days leave to amend, to provide Plaintiff with the opportunity to allege whether the contracts of sale were written, implied by conduct, or oral. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)
